title: calocephalus badmanii
intro: Calocephalus badmanii, also known as the Badman's calocephalus, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and silvery-green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits associated with Calocephalus badmanii include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as its potential use in treating skin conditions such as acne and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, with some practitioners also using it in topical applications to aid in wound healing.
